author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. Chap. v. 3 and 8, 'Blessed are the poor in spirit:' etc., but especially on that, 'Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.'

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. Chap. v. 3 and 8, 'Blessed are the poor in spirit:' etc., but especially on that, 'Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.'

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the return of the commemoration of a holy virgin, who gave her testimony to Christ, and was found worthy of a testimony from Christ, who was put to death openly, and crowned invisibly, I am reminded to speak to you, beloved, on that exhortation which the Lord hath just now uttered out of the Gospel, assuring us that there are many sources of a blessed life, which there is not a man that does not wish for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is not a man surely can be found, who does not wish to be blessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But oh! if as men desire the reward, so they would not decline the work that leads to it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who would not run with all alacrity, were it told him, “Thou shalt be blessed”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him then also give a glad and ready ear when it is said, “Blessed, if thou shalt do thus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the contest be declined, if the reward be loved; and let the mind be enkindled to an eager execution of the work, by the setting forth of the reward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What we desire, and wish for, and seek, will be hereafter; but what we are ordered to do for the sake of that which will be hereafter, must be now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Begin now, then, to recall to mind the divine sayings, and the precepts and rewards of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The kingdom of heaven shall be thine hereafter; be poor in spirit now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou that the kingdom of heaven should be thine hereafter?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look well to thyself whose thou art now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be poor in spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You ask me, perhaps, “What is to be poor in spirit?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one who is puffed up is poor in spirit; therefore he that is lowly is poor in spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The kingdom of heaven is exalted; but “he who humbleth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark what follows: “Blessed,” saith He, “are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wishest to possess the earth now; take heed lest thou be possessed by it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou be meek, thou wilt possess it; if ungentle, thou wilt be possessed by it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou hearest of the proposed reward, do not, in order that thou mayest possess the earth, unfold the lap of covetousness, whereby thou wouldest at present possess the earth, to the exclusion even of thy neighbour by whatever means; let no such imagination deceive thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then wilt thou truly possess the earth, when thou dost cleave to Him who made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is to be meek, not to resist thy God, that in that thou doest well He may be well-pleasing to thee, not thou to thyself; and in that thou sufferest ill justly, He may not be unpleasing to thee, but thou to thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no small matter is it that thou shalt be well-pleasing to Him, when thou art displeased with thyself; whereas if thou art well-pleased with thine own self, thou wilt be displeasing to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend to the third lesson, “Blessed are they that mourn, for they shall be comforted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The work consisteth in mourning, the reward in consolation; for they who mourn in a carnal sort, what consolations have they?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Miserable consolations, objects rather of fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There the mourner is comforted by things which make him fear lest he have to mourn again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For instance, the death of a son causes the father sorrow, and the birth of a son joy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one he has carried out to his burial, the other he has brought into the world; in the former is occasion of sadness, in the latter of fear: and so in neither is there consolation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That therefore will be the true consolation, wherein shall be given that which may not be lost, so that they may rejoice for their after consolation, who mourn that they are in exile now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us come to the fourth work and its reward, “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou desire to be filled?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the flesh long for fulness, after digestion thou wilt suffer hunger again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So He saith, “Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the remedy which is applied to a wound heal it, there is no more pain; but that which is applied against hunger, food that is, is so applied as to give relief only for a little while.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the fulness is past, hunger returns.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This remedy of fulness is applied day by day, yet the wound of weakness is not healed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us therefore “hunger and thirst after righteousness, that we may be filled” with that righteousness after which we now hunger and thirst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For filled we shall be with that for which we hunger and thirst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let our inner man then hunger and thirst, for it hath its own proper meat and drink.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I,” saith He, “am the Bread which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here is the bread of the hungry; long also for the drink of the thirsty, “For with Thee is the well of life.”)

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark what comes next: “Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do this, and so shall it be done to thee; deal so with others, that God may so deal with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou art at once in abundance and in want—in abundance of temporal things, in want of things eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The man whom thou hearest is a beggar, and thou art thyself God’s beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Petition is made to thee, and thou makest thy petition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As thou hast dealt with thy petitioner, so shall God deal with His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art at once full and empty; fill the empty with thy fulness, that thy emptiness may be filled with the fulness of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark what comes next: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the end of our love; an end whereby we are perfected, and not consumed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is an end of food, and an end of a garment; of food when it is consumed by the eating; of a garment when it is perfected in the weaving.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both the one and the other have an end; but the one is an end of consumption, the other of perfection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever we now do, whatsoever we now do well, whatsoever we now strive for, or are in laudable sort eager for, or blamelessly desire, when we come to the vision of God, we shall require no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what need he seek for, with whom God is present? or what shall suffice him, whom God sufficeth not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We wish to see God, we seek, we kindle with desire to see Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who doth not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But mark what is said: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Provide thyself then with that whereby thou mayest see Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For (to speak after the flesh) how with weak eyes desirest thou the rising of the sun?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the eye be sound, and that light will be a rejoicing, if it be not sound, it will be but a torment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not permitted with a heart impure to see that which is seen only by the pure heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt be repelled, driven back from it, and wilt not see it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How often already hath he enumerated the blessed, and the causes of their blessedness, and their works and recompenses, their merits and rewards!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But nowhere hath it been said, “They shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that mourn, for they shall be comforted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are the merciful, they shall obtain mercy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In none of these hath it been said, “They shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we come to the “pure in heart,” there is the vision of God promised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And not without good cause; for there, in the heart, are the eyes, by which God is seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Speaking of these eyes, the Apostle Paul saith, “The eyes of your heart being enlightened.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At present then these eyes are enlightened, as is suitable to their infirmity, by faith; hereafter as shall be suited to their strength, they shall be enlightened by sight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For as long as we are in the body we are absent from the Lord; For we walk by faith, not by sight.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now as long as we are in this state of faith, what is said of us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We see now through a glass darkly; but then face to face.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no thought be entertained here of a bodily face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if enkindled by the desire of seeing God, thou hast made ready thy bodily face to see Him, thou wilt be looking also for such a face in God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if now thy conceptions of God are at least so spiritual as not to imagine Him to be corporeal (of which subject I treated yesterday at considerable length, if yet it was not in vain), if I have succeeded in breaking down in your heart, as in God’s temple, that image of human form; if the words in which the Apostle expresses his detestation of those, “who, professing themselves to be wise became fools, and changed the glory of the incorruptible God into an image made like unto corruptible man,” have entered deep into your minds, and taken possession of your inmost heart; if ye do now detest and abhor such impiety, if ye keep clean for the Creator His own temple, if ye would that He should come and make His abode with you, “Think of the Lord with a good heart, and in simplicity of heart seek for Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark well who it is to whom ye say, if so be ye do say it, and say it in sincerity, “My heart said to Thee, I will seek Thy face.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let thine heart also say, and add, “Thy face, Lord, will I seek.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so wilt thou seek it well, because thou seekest with thine heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Scripture speaks of the “face of God, the arm of God, the hands of God, the feet of God, the seat of God,” and His footstool; but think not in all this of human members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wouldest be a temple of truth, break down the idol of falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The hand of God is His power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The face of God is the knowledge of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The feet of God are His presence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The seat of God, if thou art so minded, is thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps thou wilt venture to deny that Christ is God!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Not so,” you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou grant this too, that “Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I grant it,” you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear then, “The soul of the righteous is the seat of wisdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For where hath God His seat, but where He dwelleth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And where doth He dwell, but in His temple?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take heed therefore how thou dost receive God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “God is a Spirit, and must be worshipped in spirit and in truth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the ark of testimony enter now into thy heart, if thou art so minded, and let Dagon fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now therefore give ear at once, and learn to long for God; learn to make ready that whereby thou mayest see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed,” saith He, “are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou make ready the eyes of the body?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He should be seen by them, that which should be so seen would be contained in space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He who is wholly everywhere is not contained in space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cleanse that whereby He may be seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear and understand, if haply through His help I shall be able to explain it; and may He help us to the understanding of all the above-named works and rewards, how suitable rewards are apportioned to their corresponding duties.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For where is there anything said of a reward which does not suit, and harmonize with its work?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the lowly seem as it were aliens from a kingdom, He saith, “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because meek men are easily despoiled of their land, He saith, “Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the land.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the rest are plain at once; they are understood of themselves, and require no one to treat of them at length; they need only one to mention them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that mourn.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what mourner does not desire consolation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They,” saith He, “shall be comforted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hungry and thirsty man does not seek to be filled?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And they,” saith He, “shall be filled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed are the merciful.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What merciful man but wishes that a return should be rendered him by God of His own work, that it may be so done to him, as he doeth to the poor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Blessed,” saith He, “are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How in each case hath every duty its appropriate reward: and nothing is introduced in the reward which doth not suit the precept!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the precept is, that thou be “poor in spirit;” the reward, that thou shalt have the “kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The precept is, that thou be “meek;” the reward, that thou shalt “possess the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The percept is, that thou “mourn;” the reward, that thou shalt be “comforted.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The precept is, that thou “hunger and thirst after righteousness;” the reward, that thou shalt “be filled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The precept is, that thou be “merciful;” the reward, that thou shalt “obtain mercy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so the precept is, that thou cleanse the heart; the reward, that thou shalt see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do not so conceive of these precepts and rewards, as to think when thou dost hear, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God,” that the poor in spirit, or the meek, or they that mourn, or they who hunger and thirst after righteousness, or the merciful, will not see Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think not of those that are pure in heart, that they only will see Him, whilst the others will be excluded from the sight of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all these several characters are the self-same persons.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They shall all see; but they shall not see in that they are poor in spirit, or meek, or in that they mourn, and hunger and thirst after righteousness, or are merciful, but in that they are pure in heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as if bodily works were duly assigned to the several members of the body, and one were to say for example, Blessed are they who have feet, for they shall walk; blessed are they that have hands, for they shall work; blessed are they that have a voice, for they shall cry aloud; blessed are they who have a mouth and tongue, for they shall speak; blessed are they that have eyes, for they shall see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even so our Lord arranging in their order the members as it were of the soul, hath taught what is proper to each.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Humility qualifies for the possession of the kingdom of heaven; meekness qualifies for possessing the earth; mourning for consolation; hunger and thirst after righteousness for being filled; mercy for the obtaining mercy; a pure heart for seeing God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then we desire to see God, whereby shall our eye be purified?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who would not care for, and diligently seek the means of purifying that eye whereby he may see Him whom he longeth after with an entire affection?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Divine record has expressly mentioned this when it says, “purifying their hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The faith of God then purifies the heart, the pure heart sees God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because this faith is sometimes so defined by men who deceive themselves, as though it were enough only to believe (for some promise themselves even the sight of God and the kingdom of heaven, who believe and live evilly); against these, the Apostle James, incensed and indignant as it were with a holy charity, saith in his Epistle, “Thou believest there is one God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou applaudest thyself for thy faith, for thou markest how that many ungodly men think there are gods many, and thou rejoicest in thyself because thou dost believe that there is but one God; “Thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall they also see God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They shall see Him who are pure in heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But who can say that unclean spirits are pure in heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet they also “believe and tremble.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our faith then must be different from the faith of devils.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our faith purifies the heart; but their faith makes them guilty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they do wickedly, and therefore say they to the Lord, “What have we to do with Thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou hearest the devils say this, thinkest thou that they do not acknowledge Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We know,” they say, “who Thou art: Thou art the Son of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Peter says, and is commended; the devil says it, and is condemned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence cometh this, but that though the words be the same, the heart is different?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then make a distinction in our faith, and not be content to believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is no such faith as purifieth the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Purifying their hearts,” it is said, “by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by what, and what kind of faith, save that which the Apostle Paul defines when he says, “Faith which worketh by love.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That faith distinguishes us from the faith of devils, and from the infamous and abandoned conduct of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Faith,” he says.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That which worketh by love,” and which hopeth for what God doth promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing is more exact or perfect than this definition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are then in faith these three things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He in whom that faith is which worketh by love, must necessarily hope for that which God doth promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hope therefore is the associate of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For hope is necessary as long as we see not what we believe, lest perhaps through not seeing, and by despairing to see, we fail.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we see not, doth make us sad; but that we hope we shall see, comforteth us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hope then is here, and she is the associate of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then charity also, by which we long, and strive to attain, and glow with desire, and hunger and thirst.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then is taken in also; and so there will be faith, hope, and charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how shall there not be charity there, since charity is nothing else but love?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this faith is itself defined as that “which worketh by love.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away faith, and all thou believest perisheth; take away charity, and all that thou dost perisheth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is the province of faith to believe, of charity to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou believest without love, thou dost not apply thyself to good works; or if thou dost, it is as a servant, not as a son, through fear of punishment, not through love of righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore I say, that faith purifieth the heart, which worketh by love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what does this faith effect at present?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does it by so many testimonies of Scripture, by its manifold lessons, its various and plentiful exhortations, but make us “see now through a glass darkly, and hereafter face to face.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But return not now in thought again to this thy bodily face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think only of the face of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Force, compel, press thine heart to think of things divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever occurs to thy mind that is like to a body, throw it off from thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou canst not yet say, “It is this,” yet at least say, “It is not this.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when wilt thou be able to say, “This is God”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not even then, when thou shall see Him; for what thou shalt then see is ineffable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus the Apostle says, that he “was caught up into the third heaven, and heard ineffable words.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the words are ineffable, what is He whose words they are?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore as thou dost think of God, perchance there is presented to thee the idea of some human figure of marvellous and exceeding greatness, and thou hast set it before the eyesof thy mind as something very great, and grand, and of vast extension.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Still somewhere thou hast set bounds to it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hast, it is not God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou hast not set bounds to it, where can the face be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art fancying to thyself some huge body, and in order to distinguish the members in it, thou must needs set bounds to it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in no other way but by setting bounds to this large body, canst thou distinguish the members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what art thou about, O foolish and carnal imagination!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast made a large bulky body, and so much the larger, as thou hast thought the more to honour God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another adds one cubit to it, and makes it greater than before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “I have read,” you will say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hast thou read, who hast understood nothing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet tell me, what hast thou read?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not thrust back the babe in understanding with his play.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me, what hast thou read?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Heaven is My throne, and the earth is My footstool.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I hear thee; I have read it also: but it may be that thou thinkest thyself to have the advantage, in that thou hast both read and believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I also believe what thou hast just said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then believe it together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do I say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us search it out together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! hold fast what thou hast so read and believed; “Heaven is My throne (that is, “my seat,” for “throne,” in Greek, is “seat,” in Latin), and the earth is My footstool.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But hast thou not read these words as well, “Who has meted out the heaven with the palm of His hand?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I conclude that thou hast read them; thou dost acknowledge them, and confess that thou believest them; for in that book we read both the one and the other, and believe both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now think a while, and teach me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I make thee my teacher, and myself the little one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Teach me, I pray thee, “Who is He that sitteth on the palm of His hand?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, thou hast drawn the figure and lineaments of the members of God from a human body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And perhaps it has occurred to thee to think, that it is according to the body that we were made after the Image of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will admit this idea for a time to be considered, and canvassed, and examined, and by disputation to be thoroughly sifted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then, if it please thee, hear me; for I heard thee in what thou wast pleased to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God sitteth in heaven, and meteth out the heaven with His palm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! doth the same heaven become broad when it is God’s seat, and narrow, when He meteth it out?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or is God when sitting, limited to the measure of His palm?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this be so, God did not make us after His likeness, for the palm of our hand is much narrower than that part of the body whereon we sit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if He be as broad in His palm as in His sitting, He hath made our members quite unlike His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is no resemblance here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the Christian then blush to set up such an idol in his heart as this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore take heaven for all saints.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the earth also is spoken of all who are in the earth, “Let all the earth worship Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we may properly say with regard to those who dwell on the earth, “Let all the earth worship Thee,” we may with the same propriety say also as to those who dwell in heaven, “Let all the heaven bear Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For even the Saints who dwell on earth, though in their body they tread the earth, in heart dwell in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not in vain that they are reminded to “lift up their hearts,” and when they are so reminded, they answer, “that they lift them up:” nor in vain is it said, “If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Set your affections on things above, not on things on the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In so far therefore as they have their conversation there, they do bear God, and they are heaven; because they are the seat of God; and when they declare the words of God, “The heavens declare the glory of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Return then with me to the face of the heart, and make it ready.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That to which God speaketh is within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The ears, and eyes, and all the rest of the visible members, are either the dwelling place or the instrument of some thing within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the inner man where Christ doth dwell, now by faith, and hereafter He will dwell in it, by the presence of His Divinity, when we shall have known “what is the length, and breadth, and depth, and height; when we shall have known also the love of Christ that surpasseth knowledge, that we may be filled with all the fulness of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then if thou wouldest enter into the meaning of these words, summon all thy powers to comprehend the breadth, and length, and height, and depth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wander not in the imagination of the thoughts through the spaces of the world, and the yet comprehensible extent of this so vast a body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look for what I am speaking of in thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The “breadth” is in good works; the “length” is in long-suffering and perseverance in well-doing; the “height” is in the expectation of rewards above, for which height’s sake thou art bidden “to lift up thy heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do well, and persevere in well-doing, because of God’s reward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Esteem earthly things as nothing, lest, when this earth shall be smitten with any scourge of that wise One, thou say that thou hast worshipped God in vain, hast done good works in vain, hast persevered in good works in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by doing good works thou hadst as it were the “breadth,” by persevering in them thou hadst as it were the “length;” but by seeking earthly things thou hast not had the “height.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now observe the “depth;” it is the grace of God in the secret dispensation of His will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been His counsellor?” and, “Thy judgments are as a great depth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This conversation of well-doing, of perseverance in well-doing, of hoping for rewards above, of the secret dispensation of the grace of God, in wisdom not in foolishness, nor yet in finding fault, because one man is after this manner and another after that; for “there is no iniquity with God;” apply this, I say, if you think good, also to the Cross of thy Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was not without a meaning that He chose this kind of death, in whose power it was even either to die or not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if it was in His power to die or not, why was it not in His power also to die in this or the other manner!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not without a meaning then did He select the Cross, whereby to crucify thee to this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the “breadth” is the transverse beam in the cross where the hands are fastened, to signify good works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The “length” is in that part of the wood which reaches from this transverse beam to the ground.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there the body is crucified and in a manner stands, and this standing signifies perseverance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now “the height” is in that part, which from the same transverse beam projects upward to the head, and hereby is signified the expectation of things above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And where is the “depth” but in that part which is fixed in the ground?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so is the dispensation of grace, hidden and in secret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not seen itself, but from thence is projected all that is seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After this, when thou shalt have comprehended all these things, not in the mere understanding but in action also (“for a good understanding have all they that do hereafter),” then if thou canst, stretch out thyself to attain to the knowledge of the “love of Christ which passeth knowledge.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou hast attained to it, thou “wilt be filled with all the fulness of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then will be fulfilled the “face to face.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou wilt be filled with all the fulness of God, not as if God should be full of thee, but so that thou shalt be full of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek there, if thou canst, for any bodily face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Away with such trifles from the eye of the mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the child cast away his playthings, and learn to handle more serious matters.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in many things we are but children; and when we were more so than we are, we were borne with by our betters.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by this is the heart purified; for that in it is that faith “which worketh by love.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hence, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”
